idea.hook: You know Proxima Centauri is far. But what about the *next* closest star with a planet?
facts: Barnard's Star b is approximately 6 light-years away, or 56 TRILLION kilometers.
Traveling at the speed of light (299,792 km/s), it would still take you 6 years to reach it.
Our fastest probe, Parker Solar Probe, hit a record 635,266 km/h (0.058% light speed).
idea.payoff: At that speed, reaching Barnard's Star b would take over 10 MILLION years! Interstellar travel is a mind-boggling challenge, even to our nearest exoplanet neighbors!
idea.title: Travel to Barnard's Star b! 🚀⏳🤯

scenes.visual: Distant view of a red dwarf star (Proxima Centauri) against a dark, starry background. Faint glow.

scenes.text: Barnard's Star b: 6 light-years (56 TRILLION km)
scenes.visual: Zoom into Barnard's Star b, showing the star and its exoplanet orbiting. Distance indicator line appears.

scenes.text: Light speed (299,792 km/s): 6 YEARS to travel
scenes.visual: Stylized beam of light streaking from left to right across vast space. A digital clock counts '6 YEARS'.

scenes.text: Fastest probe (Parker Solar Probe): 0.058% light speed.
scenes.visual: Animation of the Parker Solar Probe flying at extreme speed past a stylized Sun. A percentage counter '0.058%' appears.

scenes.text: OVER 10 MILLION YEARS! A mind-boggling challenge.
scenes.visual: Vast, empty space with a huge, flashing number '10,000,000' then 'YEARS'. Fades to a tiny distant star, emphasizing scale.
